-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","title":"EPIC: Agent Orchestration Layer (Agent Mail + artifact compiler)","description":"# Goal
Implement the multi-agent loop as an **orchestrator** that coordinates *operator-run* CLI agents via Agent Mail.

This layer does NOT call model APIs. Its job is:
- turn a kickoff (template + excerpt + optional memory) into role-specific prompts
- send prompts to agents via Agent Mail threads
- track acknowledgements + responses
- parse structured deltas
- compile/merge into canonical artifacts
- publish compiled artifacts back to the same thread

---

# Key constraint
Agents are humans-in-the-loop tools:
- Codex CLI / Claude Code / Gemini CLI run in terminal sessions (often in an `ntm` tmux cockpit).
- They communicate results back by posting messages to Agent Mail.

So: orchestration is *message passing + compilation*, not remote inference.

---

# The thread contract
- **Agent Mail `thread_id` is the join key**. Prefer using the Beads ID as the thread ID for engineering work.
- The orchestrator must be able to reconstruct session state from thread messages.

We need a lightweight message taxonomy (subjects or frontmatter) so compilation is deterministic:
- kickoff
- agent delta response
- compiled artifact
- critique round
- operator/admin notes

---

# Definition of done (for this epic)
- Kickoff sending is deterministic and role-aware.
- Thread status can be computed (who responded, latest artifact, missing acks).
- Compiler produces a canonical artifact that passes lint rules.
- Compiled artifact can be published back to the Agent Mail thread.
- Persistence (optional) is explicit, minimal-sprawl, and keyed by thread id.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.379969053-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:02:37.09154375-05:00","labels":["agent-mail","orchestration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:21:57.381001437-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","title":"EPIC: Agent Orchestration Layer (Agent Mail + artifact compiler)","description":"# Goal
Implement the multi-agent loop as an **orchestrator** that coordinates *operator-run* CLI agents via Agent Mail.

This layer does NOT call model APIs. Its job is:
- turn a kickoff (template + excerpt + optional memory) into role-specific prompts
- send prompts to agents via Agent Mail threads
- track acknowledgements + responses
- parse structured deltas
- compile/merge into canonical artifacts
- publish compiled artifacts back to the same thread

---

# Key constraint
Agents are humans-in-the-loop tools:
- Codex CLI / Claude Code / Gemini CLI run in terminal sessions (often in an `ntm` tmux cockpit).
- They communicate results back by posting messages to Agent Mail.

So: orchestration is *message passing + compilation*, not remote inference.

---

# The thread contract
- **Agent Mail `thread_id` is the join key**. Prefer using the Beads ID as the thread ID for engineering work.
- The orchestrator must be able to reconstruct session state from thread messages.

We need a lightweight message taxonomy (subjects or frontmatter) so compilation is deterministic:
- kickoff
- agent delta response
- compiled artifact
- critique round
- operator/admin notes

---

# Definition of done (for this epic)
- Kickoff sending is deterministic and role-aware.
- Thread status can be computed (who responded, latest artifact, missing acks).
- Compiler produces a canonical artifact that passes lint rules.
- Compiled artifact can be published back to the Agent Mail thread.
- Persistence (optional) is explicit, minimal-sprawl, and keyed by thread id.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1","title":"Agent Mail: integration hardening (client + contracts + errors)","description":"# Goal
Make Agent Mail integration robust and shared across web + CLI.

Scope:
- standardize client behavior (URL/path/token)
- document which MCP tools/resources we depend on
- handle non-JSON/SSE responses gracefully
- improve error messages (operator-friendly)
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:24:23.277945297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:14:56.643142581-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:14:56.643142581-05:00","close_reason":"All children complete","labels":["agent-mail","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:24:23.298271028-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1.1","title":"Agent Mail: document tool/resource contracts we rely on","description":"# Goal
Document the Agent Mail MCP contracts Brenner Bot relies on, including:
- which MCP tools we call
- which resources we read (and their URI templates)
- encoding/quoting pitfalls (e.g. URL-encode absolute paths in query params)
- fail-soft vs fail-closed behavior by context (public web vs lab vs CLI)

---

# Minimum inventory
Tools:
- ensure_project
- register_agent
- send_message
- fetch_inbox
- mark_message_read
- acknowledge_message

Resources (templates):
- resource://agents/{project_key}
- resource://thread/{thread_id}?project=...
- resource://inbox/{agent}?project=...&limit=...
- plus any views we use (ack-required, urgent-unread)

---

# Gotchas to capture
- When passing an absolute path as a query param value (e.g. project=/data/...): URL-encode it (project=%2Fdata%2F...).
- Some servers may respond via SSE; client should present actionable errors.

---

# Acceptance
- A written contract exists that a future contributor can implement against.
- All callers can share a small URI-builder helper with proper encoding.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-twm","title":"Task: Integrate search trigger in header/nav","description":"Add search trigger points to the main navigation.

## Purpose

Users need discoverable ways to access search beyond keyboard shortcuts.

## Trigger Points

### Desktop Header
- Search icon in header nav
- Keyboard hint: '⌘K' shown next to icon
- Click opens search modal

### Mobile Bottom Nav
- Search could be 5th nav item, OR
- Search icon in header (more accessible)
- Tap opens search modal (or full-screen search on mobile)

### Existing Command Palette
- Search could extend/replace current ⌘K palette
- Or be a separate feature
- Decision: Document which approach

## Design Considerations

### Desktop
- Subtle but discoverable
- Keyboard hint educates users
- Consistent with Linear/Notion pattern

### Mobile
- Must be easily reachable with thumb
- Consider search as primary mobile navigation
- Full-screen search experience may be better than modal

## Implementation

### Header Change
- Add search button component
- Wire up click handler to open search modal
- Show keyboard shortcut hint

### Mobile Navigation
- Add search to bottom nav OR header
- Ensure touch target >= 44px
- Consider navigation implications

### Global Keyboard Handler
- ⌘K / Ctrl+K opens search
- May already exist (Command Palette), need to integrate

## Files to Modify

- `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` (header)
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/nav.tsx`
- Possibly create `SearchTrigger.tsx` component

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Search icon in desktop header
- [ ] Keyboard hint displayed
- [ ] Search accessible on mobile
- [ ] Click/tap opens search modal
- [ ] ⌘K keyboard shortcut works
- [ ] Mobile trigger has proper touch target","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:28.176820007-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:23.525137757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:23.525137757-05:00","close_reason":"SearchTrigger component integrated in layout.tsx header with ⌘K shortcut, SearchProvider wraps entire app","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-twm","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-d4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:12.011339591-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-twm","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-dy2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:12.145904873-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-twy","title":"Web: method pages (operators + loop + bayesian crosswalk)","description":"# Purpose

Explain how the Brenner method works - the operators, the Brenner Loop, the implicit Bayesianism. This is educational content that helps users understand and apply the method.

# Pages

1. **/method** - Overview of the Brenner method, the Two Axioms, ten principles
2. **/method/operators** - Interactive operator palette with definitions, examples, quotes
3. **/method/loop** - The Brenner Loop visualization (step-by-step workflow)
4. **/method/bayesian** - The implicit Bayesianism mapping table

# Components Needed

- **OperatorCard** - Card for each operator (symbol, name, definition, example, §-anchor)
- **OperatorPalette** - Grid/list of all operators with filtering
- **BrennerLoopDiagram** - Animated/interactive loop visualization
- **BayesianMappingTable** - Interactive table with Brenner move → Bayesian operation
- **TenPrinciplesList** - Expandable list of the ten principles

# Design Notes

- Operators should have their symbols prominently displayed (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, etc.)
- The loop diagram should be animated to show the flow
- Tables should be sortable/filterable
- Each operator should link to relevant transcript quotes

# Why This Matters

Users who understand the method can apply it themselves. This section transforms passive readers into active practitioners.

# Depends On

- Design System Foundation
- Landing Page (for navigation)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:11:59.309598763-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:33:02.34489152-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:33:02.34489152-05:00","close_reason":"Method page complete: has loop diagram, operators grid, core principles, links to corpus/distillations","labels":["method","operators","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-twy","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.441402281-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-twy","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:20:24.383136439-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ueu","title":"Add debounce to QuoteBank search","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:14:29.387920975-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:20:28.134309693-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:20:28.134309693-05:00","close_reason":"Added 200ms debounce to QuoteBank search using useDebounce hook"}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-uk5","title":"Implement global search with command palette","description":"# Task

Create a global search experience using a command palette (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K).

# Why Command Palette

- Familiar UX pattern (VS Code, Slack, Notion, etc.)
- Fast access from anywhere
- Can search across all content types
- Can also trigger actions (not just search)

# Search Scope

Search across:
1. **Transcript sections** - by text content
2. **Distillations** - by text content
3. **Quote bank** - by quote text
4. **Operators** - by name or definition
5. **Pages** - by title (Corpus, Distillations, Method, etc.)

# UI

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔍 Search or type a command...                    ⌘K       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  RECENT                                                      │
│  📄 §42: Choice of experimental object                      │
│  📄 §87: Abundance trick                                    │
│                                                              │
│  SUGGESTIONS                                                 │
│  📄 Corpus → complete_brenner_transcript.md                 │
│  📊 Distillations → Compare models                          │
│  ⚡ Method → Operator palette                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Implementation

Use `cmdk` library (https://cmdk.paco.me/) - lightweight, accessible, customizable.

```tsx
import { Command } from 'cmdk';

<Command>
  <Command.Input placeholder='Search or type a command...' />
  <Command.List>
    <Command.Group heading='Recent'>
      {recentItems.map(item => (
        <Command.Item key={item.id}>{item.title}</Command.Item>
      ))}
    </Command.Group>
    // ...
  </Command.List>
</Command>
```

# Search Index

Build search index at build time:
1. Parse all markdown files
2. Extract searchable content
3. Store as JSON
4. Use client-side fuzzy search (fuse.js or similar)

# Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Cmd+K` / `Ctrl+K` - Open search
- `Escape` - Close
- `↑↓` - Navigate results
- `Enter` - Go to result
- `Cmd+Enter` - Open in new tab

# Actions (Beyond Search)

Command palette can also:
- 'Go to Corpus' → navigate
- 'Toggle dark mode' → action
- 'Start new session' → navigate + action

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command palette opens with Cmd+K
- [ ] Search returns relevant results
- [ ] Results grouped by type
- [ ] Recent searches shown
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Actions work (navigation, etc.)
- [ ] Accessible","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:55.026628542-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:02.380663599-05:00","labels":["command-palette","search","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.039014299-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:02.327651525-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-uwe","title":"Task: Implement result highlighting","description":"Highlight matched terms in search results.

## Purpose

Highlighting shows users WHY a result matched, helping them quickly identify relevant results.

## Requirements

### In Search Results
- Matched terms highlighted in snippet
- Distinct visual treatment (bold, color, background)
- Must be accessible (not color-only)

### In Document (after navigation)
- Scroll matched section into view
- Temporary highlight effect (fade after 2-3 seconds)
- Clear visual indicator of matched content

## Implementation Approach

### Snippet Highlighting
1. Search engine returns match positions
2. Component wraps matched text in <mark> or styled span
3. Style with background and/or bold

### Document Highlighting
1. URL includes search query parameter
2. Document page detects query param
3. Scroll to first match
4. Apply temporary highlight class
5. Remove highlight after timeout

## Styling

```css
.search-highlight {
  background: var(--primary) / 20%;
  border-radius: 2px;
  padding: 0 2px;
}

.temp-highlight {
  animation: highlight-fade 2s ease-out;
}

@keyframes highlight-fade {
  0% { background: var(--primary) / 40%; }
  100% { background: transparent; }
}
```

## Accessibility

- Don't rely on color alone
- Add sr-only text if needed: 'matched term'
- Ensure sufficient contrast

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Matches highlighted in result snippets
- [ ] Highlighting is accessible (not color-only)
- [ ] After navigation, match scrolled into view
- [ ] Temporary highlight on matched section
- [ ] Highlight fades naturally

 {"id":"brenner_bot-v2e","title":"Build native React document renderer with custom components","description":"# Task

Build a rich document renderer using NATIVE React/TypeScript components that fully leverage Tailwind and our design system. NOT a generic markdown viewer!

# Philosophy

The content deserves custom treatment:
- Brenner quotes should look *amazing* (not just gray italic blocks)
- Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, ✂) should be styled distinctively
- Code blocks should use our OKLCH colors
- Tables should be interactive, not HTML tables
- § anchors should be beautiful and functional

# Architecture

```
Markdown source → MDX/AST parser → Custom React components
```

1. Parse markdown at build time (or server-side)
2. Map each AST node to a custom component
3. Each component uses our design system

# Custom Components Needed

## Typography
- `<Heading level={1|2|3|4} id='anchor'>...</Heading>`
- `<Paragraph>...</Paragraph>`
- `<Lead>...</Lead>` (intro paragraphs)

## Brenner-specific
- `<BrennerQuote author='Brenner' source='§42'>...</BrennerQuote>` - Special quote styling with gradient border, attribution
- `<OperatorSymbol symbol='⊘' name='Level-split' />` - Styled operator with tooltip
- `<SectionAnchor id='§42' />` - Animated anchor with copy functionality

## Content blocks
- `<CodeBlock language='typescript'>...</CodeBlock>` - Syntax highlighting with our colors
- `<Table columns={[...]} data={[...]} />` - Interactive table with sorting, hover
- `<Callout type='insight|warning|note'>...</Callout>` - Styled callout boxes
- `<Definition term='Gedanken organism'>...</Definition>` - Glossary-style definitions

## Interactive
- `<CopyButton text='...' />` - Copy citation/text
- `<ShareButton quote='...' />` - Share to social/clipboard
- `<CollapsibleSection title='...'>...</CollapsibleSection>`

# Implementation

Use `next-mdx-remote` or `@mdx-js/mdx` to:
1. Parse markdown to AST
2. Provide custom components mapping
3. Render with our components

```tsx
import { MDXRemote } from 'next-mdx-remote/rsc';

const components = {
  h1: (props) => <Heading level={1} {...props} />,
  blockquote: (props) => <BrennerQuote {...props} />,
  // ...
};

<MDXRemote source={content} components={components} />
```

# Quote Styling Example

```tsx
function BrennerQuote({ children, source }) {
  return (
    <motion.blockquote
      className="relative my-8 border-l-4 border-primary/50 bg-card/50 p-6 
                 rounded-r-xl italic text-lg leading-relaxed"
      initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }}
      whileInView={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
    >
      <span className="absolute -left-3 -top-3 text-4xl text-primary/30">"</span>
      {children}
      {source && (
        <cite className="mt-4 block text-sm text-muted-foreground not-italic">
          — Sydney Brenner, {source}
        </cite>
      )}
    </motion.blockquote>
  );
}
```

# TOC Generation

Still generate TOC from headings, but render with our components:
- Sticky sidebar on desktop
- Collapsible on mobile  
- Scroll-spy highlighting
- Smooth scroll on click

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All markdown elements map to custom components
- [ ] Typography uses our design system
- [ ] Quotes look beautiful (not generic)
- [ ] Operators have distinctive styling
- [ ] § anchors are interactive
- [ ] TOC works with scroll-spy
- [ ] Animations respect reduced motion
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:46.390974262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1 + brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.931287472-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.908825369-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.941047723-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal
Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components.

## Background
Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props:
```typescript
// TranscriptViewer.tsx
interface TranscriptViewerProps {
  data: TranscriptData;  // Passed from Server Component
}
```

This works but means:
1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation
2. No caching between page visits
3. Server must parse on every request

## Migration Strategy

### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended)
Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary.
- Best for SEO (content in initial HTML)
- Best for performance (no client-side loading flash)
- Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations

### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch
Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything.
- Simpler code
- Shows loading state on first visit
- Worse SEO

**We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR.

## Implementation

### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
```typescript
import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query';

export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient();
  
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc],
    queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc),
  });

  return (
    <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(queryClient)}>
      <DocumentViewer docId={params.doc} />
    </HydrationBoundary>
  );
}
```

### 2. Update TranscriptViewer
```typescript
export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) {
  const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId);
  
  if (isLoading) return <TranscriptSkeleton />;
  if (error) return <ErrorBoundary error={error} />;
  
  // ... rest of component
}
```

### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly

## Considerations

### Skeleton Components
Create loading skeletons that match the layout:
- TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders
- DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders

### Error Boundaries
Create error UI for:
- Document not found
- Parse failure
- Network error

### Backward Compatibility
During migration, support both patterns:
- Accept data as prop (old pattern)
- Use Query if docId provided (new pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML)
- [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached)
- [ ] Loading states are graceful
- [ ] Error states are handled

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx

## Files to Create
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
